subtitle: One Lantern, No Audience
location_text: An unmapped clearing — somewhere off the Acadian river, hours past where anyone would walk.
format_note: the chosen-solitude card. Mirror of XII Hanged Man (forced suspension) — Hermit elects the same stillness Hanged Man is held in.

ciphers.text: the lantern — six-sided, one panel cracked, throwing a narrow cone
ciphers.encoding_hint: the Hermit's lantern in tradition shines on the path; here it lights a cone of about six feet — the space of a single contemplation. The cracked panel means the cone leaks sideways. The figure is not looking at what the lantern shows; the figure is reading the leak.

ciphers.text: a hooked staff leaning against the figure — same wood as the Hierophant's crozier
ciphers.encoding_hint: the Hermit's staff is the Hierophant's crozier without the institution. Same wood, same hook, no congregation. The Hermit is the religious figure who has stepped out of the rite. V → IX: institutional silence → personal silence; AUDI ET TACE answered in the woods instead of the pew.

ciphers.text: a six-pointed star inside the lantern's broken panel
ciphers.encoding_hint: the Seal of Solomon — wisdom-as-light. The star is inside the lantern, not above it: the source is inside the carrier. Strength wore the lemniscate; the Hermit carries the seal. Both glyphs say the figure has internalized what other cards display.

ciphers.text: a single VHS tape labeled 'HERMIT_01' tucked into the lantern base
ciphers.encoding_hint: Elicia archives the Hermit too. The Hermit walks away from the institution; Elicia (II) follows with her tapes. The Priestess's archive is the deck's true Hermit — chosen solitude in the form of recording everyone else's. II ↔ IX is the deck's quiet pair.

ciphers.text: the figure's shadow falls upward — not forward, not back
ciphers.encoding_hint: IX ↔ XII mirror: the Hermit's shadow falls upward; the Hanged Man's body falls downward. Same vertical, opposite directions. The Hermit chose this stillness; the Hanged Man was put in it. The mirror is the deck's gentlest opposition — both figures are alone with the same dark, lit differently.

hotspots.notes: DORMANT: appears only after the player has examined Anya's tapes on the Priestess. A small cassette labeled HERMIT_01 is tucked into the lantern base — Elicia archives the Hermit too, follows him into the woods with a tape. The deck's quiet pair (II ↔ IX) renders observer and observed as the same gesture: chosen solitude in two forms.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has recognized that the bishop's crozier IS Dante's staff on the Hierophant card. The Hermit's hooked staff is the third position of the same wood — civil staff (Emperor), religious crozier (Hierophant), wandering crook (Hermit). The Hermit's authority is over himself; the staff is the same.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has observed Natalie's downward shadow on the Hanged Man card. The Hermit's upward shadow is now visibly the inverse of Natalie's downward one — same diffusion, same proportions, mirror axis. IX ↔ XII completes itself once both shadows are read; the deck's gentlest opposition closes.

ENTRY HOTSPOT: clicking the cassette tucked in the lantern base dispatches daemon_listener.02 to play back HERMIT_01 in full (47:11, integrity 0.94). The diorama renders the recording as a scrubbable waveform with seven event markers — five are non-speech (crackle, breath, sit-down, tape-ends), two are the Hermit's only spoken moments (his ten-word line about the lantern guttering, and the moment he calls out to Elicia by name). Elicia broke her no-tape agreement once to make this recording; she did not make a second tape.

easter_eggs: the Hermit's lantern shines a narrow cone — six-foot reach — the space of one held thought. Wisdom illuminates locally; tradition wants global lighting.
the crozier→staff lineage is the deck's most subtle authority chain: Emperor's secular sceptre, Hierophant's sacramental crozier, Hermit's wandering crook. Three vestments of one rod of office.
Faith is not on this card. The Hermit's chosen solitude is the only chapter where the dog is absent — the through-line animal walked elsewhere because the figure didn't ask her to come.
the upward-falling shadow is the visual rhyme with the Hanged Man card (XII). Same vertical line through the painted plane; one chose the stillness, one is held in it.
the Hermit is the Priestess's mirror in motion: she archives by sitting still, he listens by walking alone. II ↔ IX is the deck's quiet pair.

cross_references.lemniscate_alternate: strength.json::str_lemniscate_halo (both internalize a sigil others display)
